key_insight: The highest-leverage enhancements add causal ordering, conflict-free merge, and sensor fusion -- these are the three hardest distributed systems problems Anduril faces at the edge, and the current codebase has clean seams to slot them in.

findings.description: Hybrid Logical Clocks (HLC) -- Add HLC timestamps to every entity mutation so that causally-ordered merge is possible across partitioned mesh peers. Each store node maintains its own HLC; on mesh relay forward, the receiver advances its clock to max(local, remote)+1. This replaces the current timestamppb.Now() wall-clock stamps with causally meaningful ones, enabling last-writer-wins merge and conflict detection.
findings.recommendation: Priority 1, Size M. Build first -- it is a prerequisite for CRDT merge and partition tolerance. Teaches: vector clocks, hybrid logical clocks, causal ordering, Lamport timestamps. K8s analogy: ResourceVersion on every K8s object -- the optimistic concurrency field that etcd uses to reject stale writes.

findings.description: CRDT Component Merge -- Implement conflict-free merge for entity components using operation-based CRDTs. Position/velocity use last-writer-wins register (keyed on HLC), classification uses max-threat-wins, task catalog uses OR-set (add wins over remove). The mesh relay becomes a CRDT replication layer: on receive, it merges component-by-component rather than wholesale overwrite. Add a partition simulation mode that splits peers, lets them diverge, then reconnects and verifies convergence.
findings.location: internal/mesh/relay.go (merge logic), new internal/crdt/ package, proto/entity/v1/entity.proto (version vectors per component)
findings.recommendation: Priority 1, Size L. The single most interview-relevant enhancement -- Anduril's Lattice must merge entity state from disconnected edge nodes without coordination. Build merge functions per component type, add property-based tests proving convergence. Teaches: CRDTs, eventual consistency, conflict resolution strategies. K8s analogy: server-side apply's field-manager merge -- each component owner merges independently, conflicts resolved by policy.

findings.description: Sensor Fusion / Track Correlation -- Add a second sensor-sim instance producing overlapping tracks with different IDs, noise, and update rates. Build a fusion service that correlates tracks from multiple sensors into a single fused entity using nearest-neighbor gating on position/velocity. Fused tracks get a new FusionComponent listing contributing sensor track IDs and a covariance estimate. This directly maps to Lattice's multi-sensor track correlation problem.
findings.location: new internal/fusion/ package, new cmd/fusion/ binary, extends proto/entity/v1/entity.proto with FusionComponent and SensorSourceComponent
findings.recommendation: Priority 1, Size L. Extremely high interview signal -- sensor fusion is core Lattice IP. Start simple (Euclidean distance gating), then optionally add a Kalman filter for position prediction. Teaches: track correlation, sensor fusion, measurement uncertainty, data association. K8s analogy: endpoint slices merging pod IPs from multiple node sources into a single Service abstraction.

findings.description: Partition Tolerance and Disconnect Resilience -- Add a local write-ahead log (WAL) to each store node so that when mesh peers are unreachable, mutations queue locally and replay on reconnect. Implement anti-entropy protocol: on reconnection, peers exchange entity version summaries (Merkle tree or version vector digest) and sync only the delta. Add a chaos mode to mesh-relay that randomly drops connections for N seconds.
findings.location: internal/mesh/relay.go (queue + reconnect), new internal/wal/ package, new internal/antientropy/ package
findings.recommendation: Priority 2, Size L. Demonstrates deep understanding of edge-native constraints -- intermittent connectivity is Anduril's daily reality. Teaches: write-ahead logging, anti-entropy, Merkle trees, partition detection and recovery. K8s analogy: kubelet's standalone mode -- when the node loses API server connectivity, it keeps running pods from its local checkpoint and reconciles when connectivity returns.

findings.description: Human-on-the-Loop Approval Gate -- Add an approval workflow to the task-manager: when threat escalates to HIGH and task state transitions to 'intercept', the task enters a 'pending_approval' state. An operator must approve via lattice-cli before the intercept tasks activate. Implement a timeout-based auto-escalation if no human responds within N seconds. Wire this through a new ApprovalComponent on the entity.
findings.recommendation: Priority 2, Size M. Directly relevant to Anduril's autonomy philosophy -- autonomous systems with human oversight gates. Teaches: human-in-the-loop control systems, state machine design, approval workflows, timeout escalation. K8s analogy: admission webhooks -- a validating webhook that gates resource creation on external approval before the object is persisted.

findings.description: Distributed Tracing and Observability -- Instrument the full entity lifecycle with OpenTelemetry traces: sensor-sim creates a span on entity creation, classifier adds a child span for classification, task-manager adds a child span for task assignment. Propagate trace context through gRPC metadata. Add Prometheus metrics: entity count by type, classification latency p99, mesh replication lag. Export to stdout/OTLP collector.
findings.location: All cmd/ binaries and internal/ packages, new internal/observability/ package
findings.recommendation: Priority 3, Size M. Shows production-readiness thinking. Demonstrates understanding of distributed system debuggability -- critical when things fail at the edge. Teaches: distributed tracing, context propagation, metrics instrumentation, SLI/SLO thinking. K8s analogy: kube-apiserver audit logging + the tracing KEP -- every request gets a trace ID that flows through etcd, admission, and controllers.

findings.description: Behavior Tree Engine for Autonomous Response -- Replace the static Rules() function in task-manager with a behavior tree evaluator. Define trees in a simple DSL (YAML or protobuf): sequence nodes, selector nodes, condition checks (threat level, speed, proximity to geo-fence), and action leaves (assign task, escalate, notify). This makes the autonomy logic composable and testable without code changes.
findings.location: internal/task/manager.go (replace Rules()), new internal/behaviortree/ package, deploy/ (example tree YAML)
findings.recommendation: Priority 3, Size L. Behavior trees are the standard for game AI and autonomous systems -- Anduril uses them in their autonomy engine. Teaches: behavior trees, declarative policy engines, composable decision logic. K8s analogy: OPA/Gatekeeper policy engine -- declarative rules evaluated against resource state, composable and hot-reloadable without redeploying the controller.

findings.description: Resource-Constrained Edge Mode -- Add a configurable memory budget to the entity store. When the budget is exceeded, evict lowest-priority entities (NONE threat first, then by staleness). Add bandwidth throttling to mesh-relay: batch updates into periodic sync windows rather than streaming every mutation. Add entity delta compression -- only send changed components rather than full entity snapshots.
findings.location: internal/store/store.go (eviction policy), internal/mesh/relay.go (batching + delta), proto/store/v1/store.proto (delta update RPC)
findings.recommendation: Priority 3, Size M. Shows awareness of edge deployment constraints -- Lattice runs on drones and small form-factor hardware. Teaches: resource-constrained systems, eviction policies, delta compression, bandwidth optimization. K8s analogy: kubelet eviction manager -- when node resources are exhausted, pods are evicted by priority class, with graceful degradation rather than crash.

recommendation: Build in this order: (1) HLC timestamps -- small, foundational, unblocks everything else. (2) CRDT component merge -- the crown jewel, directly addresses Anduril's hardest problem. (3) Sensor fusion -- high interview signal, demonstrates domain understanding. (4) Human-on-the-loop approval -- quick win, shows autonomy philosophy alignment. (5) Partition tolerance with WAL -- builds on HLC+CRDT foundation. (6) Observability -- production polish. (7-8) Behavior trees and edge mode are stretch goals. The first three alone would make an extremely strong interview artifact.
